title: Finviz Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Finviz Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Finviz (4.5★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Finviz — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Finviz?

Finviz is a powerhouse stock screening platform that has been helping traders identify market opportunities since 2007. With a 4.5/5 rating among active traders, it combines an extensive database of fundamental and technical filters with iconic market heat maps that deliver instant visual insights into sector performance and market cap distribution. The platform offers a generous free tier packed with 60+ screening filters, insider trading data, and news aggregation—making it one of the few professional-grade tools that doesn't require a credit card to get started. Built for swing traders, value investors, and fundamental analysts who want speed and depth without complexity, Finviz excels at doing one thing exceptionally well: screening stocks faster than nearly any competitor.

How to Create Your Finviz Account

Getting started with Finviz takes less than five minutes and requires minimal information. Here's the process:

  • Visit Finviz.com — Go directly to the homepage and locate the "Sign Up" button in the top right corner.
  • Choose your account type — Select either Free or Elite. You can always upgrade later, so starting with Free is risk-free.
  • Enter your email address — Finviz requires a valid email for account creation and login recovery.
  • Create a password — Choose a strong password (mixed case, numbers, and special characters recommended for security).
  • Agree to terms — Review Finviz's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, then check the boxes to proceed.
  • Verify your email — Check your inbox for a confirmation email from Finviz. Click the verification link within 24 hours.
  • Log in — Once verified, return to Finviz.com and log in with your email and password.

No phone number, ID verification, or brokerage account connection is required for either the Free or Elite tier. This makes Finviz one of the fastest platforms to get running—estimated setup time is 3-5 minutes total.

Setting Up Finviz for the First Time

After logging in, you'll land on the screener dashboard. Here's how to configure it for your trading style:

Customize your dashboard layout — The default view shows the stock screener, but you can pin shortcuts to Market Overview, Heat Map, and News to your personal dashboard. Click the pin icon next to each section to save it.

Set your data refresh preferences — On the Free tier, data is delayed 15-20 minutes; on Elite, it's real-time. Be aware of this difference when setting alerts and placing trades based on screener results.

Select your home market overview — By default, Finviz shows S&P 500 stocks. If you trade small-cap or mid-cap stocks, navigate to Screener settings and change your default universe to Russell 2000 or your preferred segment.

Configure watchlist folders — Create folders for different strategies (e.g., "Dividend Plays," "Earnings Momentum," "Oversold"). You can add stocks directly from search results or screener output with one click.

Review the broker integration options — Finviz doesn't directly connect to brokers for order execution, but it does provide links to charts on TradingView and detailed fundamentals. Bookmark these integration points if you use them.

Familiarize yourself with the main navigation — The top menu includes Screener, Heat Map, Overview, Crypto, Forex, Futures, and News. You don't need to explore all of these immediately—focus on Screener and Heat Map first.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. The Stock Screener (60+ Filters)

This is Finviz's core strength. The screener lets you filter by 60+ criteria including price, volume, market cap, dividend yield, P/E ratio, technical indicators, and custom ranges. You can layer up to 15 filters simultaneously. For example, find stocks with P/E below 15, dividend yield above 3%, and price above their 200-day moving average in under a second. Results appear instantly with zero lag, and you can export your results as a CSV for further analysis.

2. Heat Maps for Visual Market Overview

Finviz's signature feature displays the entire stock market (or sectors, or industries) as a grid of colored squares representing stock performance. Green indicates gainers, red indicates losers, and the size of each square reflects market cap. Hover over any square to see the stock's price, percentage change, and sector. This visual approach is infinitely faster than reading news headlines to understand what's moving in the market.

3. Insider Trading Data

View insider buy and sell activity for any stock directly within Finviz. This data is included free and updated daily. Filter insider activity by date, insider type (officer, director, 10% owner), and transaction type. Many swing traders use insider buying as a contrarian signal when institutions are accumulating quietly before rallies.

4. News Aggregation Feed

Finviz aggregates financial news from dozens of sources and displays it with sentiment scoring. Click on any stock symbol to see recent news stories, earnings announcements, and SEC filings. The free tier includes this, making it unnecessary to juggle multiple news tabs while screening.

5. Backtesting (Elite Only)

Elite subscribers can backtest screener filters against historical data to see how a set of criteria would have performed over the past 1, 5, or 10 years. This is useful for validating whether your "value stock" filter actually beat the market historically. It's a simplified tool compared to dedicated backtesting platforms like Backtrader, but it's integrated directly into your screening workflow.

6. Real-Time Alerts (Elite Only)

Set price alerts, screening alerts (e.g., "notify me when a stock breaks above its 50-day moving average"), or news alerts for any ticker. Elite members receive alerts via email and in-app notifications with minimal delay. On the Free tier, you can check your saved stocks manually, but you won't get automatic notifications.

7. API Access (Elite Only)

If you're a developer or automate your trading research, Elite includes API access to pull screening data, heat map data, and quotes programmatically. The API is REST-based and well-documented, making it easy to integrate Finviz data into custom Python scripts or trading bots.

Finviz Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Plan — $0/month

The Free tier includes the full stock screener with 60+ filters, heat maps, insider trading data, and news aggregation. The trade-off is a 15-20 minute data delay on quotes and the absence of real-time alerts and backtesting. For beginning traders and those still learning screener mechanics, this is genuinely sufficient. Millions of traders use the Free tier daily without upgrading.

Elite Plan — $39.50/month

Elite removes the data delay (real-time quotes on stocks and futures), unlocks backtesting, enables real-time alerts via email and app, and grants API access for automation. If you're managing an active swing trading strategy or running screeners throughout market hours, the real-time component is worth the cost. At $39.50/month, it's also among the cheapest professional stock screening subscriptions available.

Who should use Free? Beginners screening for weekly or monthly trades; end-of-day swing traders who don't mind 20-minute delays; value investors screening for long-term holdings; traders using Finviz alongside a real-time broker terminal.

Who should use Elite? Day traders and active swing traders executing intraday; traders building screening automation; anyone backtesting new filter ideas before committing capital; traders who want to know instantly when a stock meets their criteria during market hours.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Finviz

1. Use Heat Maps before the Screener — Before diving into filtering, spend 30 seconds scanning the market heat map to identify which sectors are hot. Then run your screener within that sector only. This dramatically improves the quality of your results because you're fishing where the fish are moving.

2. Combine Multiple Filter Types — Finviz's filter categories span Fundamental (P/E, dividend yield), Technical (moving averages, RSI, MACD), Financial (debt-to-equity, ROE), and Valuation (price-to-book, price-to-sales). The best screeners layer across all four. For example: undervalued stocks (fundamental) with bullish technicals (technical) and strong earnings growth (financial).

3. Save Your Screener Templates — Any screener configuration can be saved by name. Create templates for different strategies: "Dividend Growth," "Oversold Bounces," "Earnings Momentum." You can then reload these in one click instead of re-entering filters each time.

4. Pair Insider Trading Data with Your Screener Results — After your screener returns candidates, click into the top 5 stocks and check insider activity from the past week. If insiders are buying, you've just added a confirming signal at zero additional cost. This trick alone filters out many low-quality signals.

5. Cross-Check Heat Map Outliers with News — When a stock's color pops dramatically on the heat map (e.g., a +15% green square in a red sector), click it to read recent news immediately. Often you'll find an earnings beat, acquisition, or guidance raise that explains the move—and validates whether it's tradeable.

6. Use the Earnings Filter in Screener — Finviz includes an earnings calendar filter. You can screen for stocks reporting earnings within the next week, or within a specific week. Combined with technical filters, this is a fast way to build an earnings-play watchlist.

7. Export CSV Results for Excel or Google Sheets Analysis — After any screener run, click "Export" to download results as CSV. Paste this into Google Sheets to sort by columns Finviz doesn't expose, add your own calculations, or maintain a rolling database of screener hits for pattern tracking.

Common Finviz Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: Free tier data feels too delayed for my trading.

Solution: Pair Finviz Free with your broker's real-time data. Use Finviz to *identify* opportunities (via screener and heat map), then confirm entry signals in real-time using your broker's terminal or TradingView's real-time charts. This hybrid approach is free and faster than upgrading.

Issue: My screener returned 500+ results and I can't tell which ones are actually good.

Solution: You've added too few filters. Finviz's power lies in *layering* constraints. Instead of 5-6 filters, try 12-15. Add technical confirmation (price above 200-day MA, RSI between 40-60). Add fundamental constraints (P/E below 20, positive cash flow). Narrow the universe to one sector. Most screeners that return this many results are too loose to be actionable.

Issue: I want to screen options, but Finviz doesn't have options screening.

Solution: Finviz intentionally focuses on stocks. If you need options screening, use thinkorswim (free, excellent options scanner) or Trade Ideas (paid, AI-driven). Finviz is incomparable for stocks but silent on options by design.

Issue: I want to screen international stocks but Finviz only covers US markets.

Solution: Finviz covers US stocks, ETFs, futures, forex, and crypto, but not foreign stock exchanges (LSE, TSE, Euronext). If you need international equity screening, check whether your target country has US-listed ADRs (American Depositary Receipts), which *are* on Finviz. For pure international stock screening, consider a dedicated service or your broker's tools.

Is Finviz Worth It? Our Verdict

Finviz is worth every penny—starting at zero. The Free tier is genuinely one of the best-kept secrets in retail trading: unlimited stock screening with 60+ filters, market heat maps, insider data, and news aggregation cost nothing. If you trade weekly or hold positions longer than a day, Free is probably sufficient. For active day traders and swing traders executing throughout market hours, the $39.50/month Elite tier is cheap insurance for real-time data and automation capabilities. Finviz's screener speed and heat maps are legitimately best-in-class—no competitor loads results faster or visualizes the market's sector rotation more intuitively. Skip it only if you're trading options or international stocks; otherwise, it belongs in your toolkit. Compare it directly with our full Finviz review or check Finviz vs TradingView if you're deciding between stock screeners.
